title: Equivariant Space Group and Hamiltonian for Collinear Magnetic Systems
authors: Chaoxi Cui, Zhi-Ming Yu, Yilin Han, Run-Wu Zhang, Shengyuan A. Yang, Yugui Yao
abstract: Condensed matter physics increasingly focuses on exploiting the magnetic order parameter orientation n as a tuning knob for properties of collinear magnetic materials, but a general method for constructing effective Hamiltonians with explicit n-dependence has been lacking. Here, we develop a symmetry-based framework, built on the equivariant space group, for constructing such Hamiltonians, termed equivariant magnetic Hamiltonians (EMHs). The resulting EMH lives in a higher-dimensional k-n space and exhibits unconventional symmetry actions and topological features. Using a 1D ferromagnetic chain and a 3D antiferromagnet as examples, we demonstrate that explicit n-dependence in EMHs enables the study of magnetic-dynamics-driven topological pumping, including even-integer charge pumping and a second-Chern-number-induced quantized pumping of surface anomalous Hall conductivity. Beyond model systems, we incorporate the framework into first-principles calculations to construct ab-initio EMHs that accurately capture the n-dependent band structures of real materials. The approach can also be generalized to non-collinear magnetic systems. Our work establishes a general framework for constructing EMHs and for exploring the rich physics arising from magnetic anisotropy and magnetic dynamics.
summary: The authors present a new framework for constructing Hamiltonians that explicitly include the direction of magnetization as a degree of freedom. This allows for the prediction of new topological phenomena, such as quantized charge pumping, driven by magnetic precession and provides a way to model real magnetic materials more accurately.
main_problem: The lack of a general, systematic method for constructing effective Hamiltonians that explicitly depend on the orientation of the magnetic order parameter in collinear magnetic materials.
main_result: The development of a symmetry-based framework called Equivariant Magnetic Hamiltonians (EMHs) that captures n-dependence, enabling the study of magnetic-dynamics-driven topological pumping and ab-initio modeling of real materials.
method: A symmetry-based construction using the Equivariant Space Group (ESG) and expanding the Hamiltonian in real spherical harmonics, supplemented by an ab-initio implementation using DFT and Wannier functions.
model_or_system: Collinear magnetic systems, including 1D ferromagnetic chains, 3D antiferromagnetic lattices (AA-stacked honeycomb), and the monolayer MnBi2Te4.
key_observables: Even-integer charge pumping, second Chern number, quantized surface anomalous Hall conductivity, and Chern-Simons angle.
important_parameters: Magnetic order parameter orientation (n), magnetic sublattice-switching factor (zeta), and expansion order (l).
main_assumptions: The expansion is truncated at low orders of l; the magnetization direction is assumed to be invariant under spatial inversion; the framework is primarily developed for collinear systems.
figures_summary: Fig 1: Symmetry comparison of square lattices; Fig 2: 1D FM chain dynamics and symmetry actions on k-n space; Fig 3: Comparison of ab-initio EMH vs DFT for MnBi2Te4; Fig 4: 3D AFM lattice structure and winding of the Chern-Simons angle.
paper_structure: Introduction of the problem; development of the Equivariant Space Group and EMH framework; application to 1D ferromagnetic pumping; extension to 3D antiferromagnetic topological features; ab-initio implementation and validation using MnBi2Te4; discussion of future non-collinear extensions.
why_interesting: The paper introduces a way to treat a continuous parameter (magnetic orientation) as a dynamical dimension, which is highly relevant for understanding many-body dynamics and topological transport driven by external or internal parameter driving.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 3. (a) Structure of monolayer MnBi2Te4. (b) Four different ˆn directions (±ˆx, ˆy, and ˆz) used in the DFT calcu- lations for extracting the U matrices. These U matrices are used to construct an ab-initio EMH. (c) Comparison between band structures obtained from this ab-initio EMH (red lines) and from direct DFT calculation (blue circles), for the mag- netization direction (θˆn, ϕˆn) = (π/4, π/3) , illustrated in the inset. (d) Enlarged view of the boxed region in (c).

FIG. 2. (a) Schematic of 1D ferromagnetic chain. (b) Pre- cession of ˆn. (c) Band structures of EMH (10) for different values of ϕˆn with θˆn = π/2. The system remains an insulator in this case. (d) The action of +T on the k-ϕˆn space, which re- sembles a glide mirror. (e) Evolution of the Zak phase γ(ϕˆn). +T symmetry requires γ having a π periodicity in ϕˆn. The red and the blue lines are the cases giving q = 2 (red) and q = 0 (blue), respectively. (f) Rotational symmetry like +C3 acts like a fractional translation on k-ϕˆn space. Here, the fundamental domain of the space (highlighted in red color) is 1/3 of the original space. For calculations in (c) and (e), we take t0 = 0, s0 = 0.2 eV,...

FIG. 4. (a) The 3D model consists of 2D honeycomb lattice stacked with AA-stacking order. The two sublattices have opposite magnetic moments, forming an antiferromagnetic or- der. (b) The intralayer hopping vectors an and bn. (c) We consider the pumping induced by the precession of ˆn with a fixed θˆn = π/4. (d) Evolution of Chern-Simons angle θCS as ϕˆn varies from 0 to 2π, exhibiting a nontrivial winding. In the calculation, we take c1 = 1 eV, c2 = −4 eV, c3 = 1.63 eV, c4 = 0.33 eV, c5 = 4.24 eV, c6 = 0.82 eV, c7 = 0.21 eV, and c8 = 1.79 eV.
